campus: Rome (Città Universitaria / Policlinico Umberto I and associated teaching hospitals)
application_platform: Universitaly (visa pre-enrolment for non-EU) and Infostud / Sapienza application portal; IMAT registration via Universitaly/CINECA
access_quota_non_eu: Annual non-EU contingent set in the Ministerial decree and Sapienza call; exact 2026-2027 figure not yet extracted from official call [uncertain]
places_and_foreign_contingent: Total places, EU and non-EU contingent set annually in the Ministerial decree and Sapienza call for the English Course F; exact 2026-2027 figures not yet published/extracted [uncertain]

academic_requirements.secondary_diploma: A valid secondary-school leaving diploma giving access to university, with at least 12 years of total schooling (or recognized equivalent); strong background in science subjects recommended.
academic_requirements.admission: Admission is restricted and conditional on passing the IMAT and ranking within available places.
academic_requirements.language: At least upper-intermediate (B2 CEFR) command of English.

country_specific_eligibility: Foreign qualifications must satisfy the equivalence/validation rules in the Sapienza/MoveIN academic requirements for 2026-2027; where fewer than 12 years of schooling, additional university credits or a foundation year may be required. Specific country rules not individually extracted [uncertain]
conditional_enrolment_policy: Candidates completing their diploma in the same year may sit the IMAT and be enrolled conditionally pending final qualification, per the national IMAT procedure [uncertain]

language_requirements.note: All students must have at least an upper-intermediate (B2) knowledge of English (or Italian depending on the chosen programme); for the English Course F the relevant language is English.
language_requirements.accepted_certificates: Standard recognized certificates (IELTS, TOEFL, Cambridge, etc.); exact accepted-certificate list and scores per the official call [uncertain]

language_submission_timing: By enrolment / as specified in the call [uncertain]
italian_language_requirement: Italian not required for admission; basic Italian recommended for clinical placements and daily life [uncertain]
application_deadline_eu: Application remains open until 31 July 2026 exclusively for EU and EU-equivalent candidates (A.Y. 2026-2027). IMAT registration follows the national schedule.
application_deadline_non_eu: Deadline for non-EU candidates requiring a visa expired on 15 May 2026 (A.Y. 2026-2027); visa pre-enrolment via Universitaly by the national deadline (typically end of June).
application_fee: Application/registration fee per Sapienza and IMAT national procedure; exact amount per official call [uncertain]
visa_preenrolment_deadline: Universitaly visa pre-enrolment by the national deadline for non-EU visa applicants (15 May 2026 for the Sapienza non-EU step)
required_documents: For foreign qualifications: Declaration of Value (DoV) or CIMEA statement, legalized/translated documents
document_translation_authentication: Foreign documents require official translation and legalization/apostille, plus a Declaration of Value (DoV) or CIMEA comparability statement, per Sapienza international procedures.

entry_exam_or_test.content: Reading Skills and General Knowledge, Logical Reasoning, Biology, Chemistry, Mathematics and Physics
entry_exam_or_test.applies_to: Both EU and non-EU applicants to English-taught Medicine; the 2025-2026 Italian Medicine 'filter semester' reform does NOT apply to English-taught programmes, so IMAT remains the selection method.
entry_exam_or_test.year_status_2026_2027: No final 2026-2027 ministerial decree confirmed at time of research; IMAT for English-taught Medicine is the expected/confirmed method per Sapienza FAQ and national sources [uncertain]

test_variant_and_session: IMAT single session per national CINECA/Universitaly schedule (autumn); exact 2026-2027 date per national decree [uncertain]
test_score_conversion_or_threshold: Admission by national/Sapienza merit ranking of IMAT scores up to available places; minimum scoring threshold per national rules [uncertain]

source_quotes.quote: The deadline for Non-EU candidates (visa applicants) expired on May 15, 2026, and the application process remains open until July 31, 2026, exclusively for EU and EU-equivalent candidates.
The IMAT admission test covers Reading Skills and General Knowledge, Biology, Chemistry, Mathematics and Physics, and Logical Reasoning.
All students must have at least an upper-intermediate knowledge of English or Italian (B2 level), depending on their chosen programme.
Some programmes require students to sit a mandatory admission test (e.g. Medicine and Surgery).

uncertainty_notes: Italy reformed Italian-taught Medicine admission for 2025-2026 (open 'filter semester' replacing entrance test). Multiple national sources state the reform does NOT apply to English-taught Medicine, where IMAT remains the selection method. No final 2026-2027 ministerial decree was confirmed at research time; IMAT is the expected method for English Course F.

Course code: task brief lists 30893 (used here); the live catalogue URL is /course/33454. The 33454 path is the current English Course F catalogue entry; the numeric code should be reconfirmed on the page.

Total places, EU/non-EU contingent, application fee, accepted-certificate list, IMAT 2026-2027 session date and threshold not extracted from the official call; mark as uncertain pending the published 2026-2027 call PDF.
official_call_url and faculty_call_pdf_url not retrieved; the 2026-2027 Medicine English call PDF should be located on farmed.web.uniroma1.it / corsidilaurea documenti_ufficiali.
